review_scope: Review whether the full-lifecycle ProductOS trace materially improves launch communication and post-release learning continuity.

target_outcomes.outcome_name: Launch communication stays attached to the canonical item trace
target_outcomes.expected_signal: Release communication should cite the same scoped item and lifecycle evidence that passed release readiness.
target_outcomes.observed_signal: The release note now points back to the same canonical lifecycle item and bounded-scope evidence chain.

target_outcomes.outcome_name: Post-release learning stays inside the same lifecycle path
target_outcomes.expected_signal: Outcome review should reuse the same lifecycle trace instead of reconstructing post-launch context from separate notes.
target_outcomes.observed_signal: The post-release review now references the same lifecycle item, readiness decision, and release communication surfaces.

claim_verification_summary: The full-lifecycle trace now keeps launch communication and post-release learning inside the same canonical item path, but external publication adapters remain a separate later slice.
adoption_notes: The lifecycle trace now covers launch preparation and outcome review without reopening hidden side channels.
The promoted slice is strong enough to keep as the stable full-lifecycle baseline.
unresolved_pain_points: External publication adapters still need to consume lifecycle traces as first-class inputs.

support_signals.summary: The lifecycle trace is easier to support because launch and post-release artifacts now reuse the same canonical item context.

adoption_signals.summary: The promoted slice is strong enough to remain the stable full-lifecycle baseline for ProductOS.

reopen_recommendations.rationale: The next bounded release should review whether publication adapters now deserve to move ahead of other adjacent workflow expansion work.

next_action: Keep the full-lifecycle trace stable and use the next bounded release to externalize publication adapters without breaking provenance.
